root_motivation: The World Ontology's IRIs are not dereferenceable and there is no W3C SPARQL Protocol endpoint; semantic-web tools (browsers, curl, SPARQLWrapper) cannot reach the lattice graph. rosetta-serve makes the joined lattice + decision graphs addressable linked data over HTTP.
user_persona: Joe Yen running wintermute locally; also any standard semantic-web client (browser, curl, Python SPARQLWrapper) pointed at 127.0.0.1:7180.

acceptance_criteria.description: rosetta-serve check --store <fixture> loads the store, prints triple count > 0, exits 0; missing/corrupt store exits non-zero with diagnostic.
SELECT query over Accept: application/sparql-results+json returns well-formed SPARQL JSON results with the requested bindings.
CONSTRUCT with Accept: text/turtle returns Turtle that round-trips through oxrdfio; same query with Accept: application/ld+json returns valid JSON-LD.
GET /wo/Dignity with Accept: text/turtle returns non-empty bounded description; with Accept: application/ld+json returns valid JSON-LD; unknown IRI returns 404.
SPARQL UPDATE (e.g. INSERT DATA) is rejected with HTTP 405 and the store is unchanged (triple count before/after).
--load of a rosetta-prov decision graph makes its prov:Activity/verdict triples queryable via /sparql (SELECT returns the loaded verdict).
A query exceeding --timeout is aborted with 5xx/diagnostic rather than hanging; subsequent requests still succeed.

scope: Store binding (--store <path>) to lattice-join oxigraph store
Startup loading of extra Turtle files (--load <ttl>…)
non_goals: SPARQL UPDATE / write operations (rejected by construction)
OWL/RDFS inference (the store already has materialized entailments from lattice-join)
Authentication / TLS (local-only; security is a deferred concern)
Full JSON-LD framing/context expansion (stub JSON-LD output is sufficient for v0.1)

five_whys_trace.a: lattice-join's oxigraph store is only reachable via a CLI in-process; its IRIs are not dereferenceable.
lattice-context's retrieval mechanism presupposes addressable subgraphs; without stable IRIs the context mechanism is incomplete.

five_whys_trace.q: Why can't ousia-mcp fill this gap?
five_whys_trace.a: ousia-mcp exposes SPARQL over MCP (agent-facing only); there is no W3C SPARQL Protocol HTTP endpoint any standard semantic-web tool could hit.

five_whys_trace.q: Why not extend lattice-join to serve HTTP?
five_whys_trace.a: lattice-join is a store-building tool; mixing HTTP serving into it violates single responsibility and the PRD model of slim focused crates.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Should empty stores exit non-zero for `check`?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Empty but openable store exits 0 with a warning; missing/corrupt exits non-zero. AC1 only requires count > 0 for the fixture test (which loads a triple).

ambiguities_resolved.question: Full JSON-LD or stub?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Stub JSON-LD (N-Triples wrapped in @graph) is sufficient for v0.1; full framing is a deferred enhancement per non-goals.
